source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Here is a Physoly round button badge, in which the logo is printed on the flat and rigid surface of this badge. Toss it in the air and track the motions of three points (indicated by cyan circles in the figure) separated a straight-line distance of $L=5 \mathrm{~mm}$ apart. At a particular moment, we find that these all have the same speed $V=4 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}$ but are heading to different directions which form an angle of $\theta=30^{\circ}$ between each pair. Determine the then angular velocity of the badge (in $\left.\mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}\right)$.
<image_1>
extraction_notes: image_1 exists as an embedded PIL image object, but no path/URL/filename is provided

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to 'the figure' and to three points 'indicated by cyan circles'
The task depends on spatial/visual configuration of tracked points on the badge, which is not fully specified in text alone
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and one image asset is available
multi_step: The question combines multiple givens (L=5 mm, V=4 cm/s, theta=30 degrees) to derive angular velocity
It involves rigid-body/kinematics reasoning across three tracked points with equal speeds but different directions
The wording suggests image-text joint reasoning: identify the point configuration from the figure, then perform a physics/geometry calculation
verifiability: A single scalar target (angular velocity in rad/s) is suitable for rule-based or formula-based checking
Multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward final-answer verification

decision_reasons: All core assets are present: question text, numeric answer, and the required image asset.
The registry passed with no recorded issues, so there is no asset-level gap blocking recovery or use.
Although the problem is strongly image-dependent, the image is available, so the key diagram-based semantics are preserved.
Initial scoring is strong overall, especially for verifiability and image-grounded reasoning, supporting inclusion in the candidate pool.
